question_text: A) Which compound is more susceptible to electrophilic aromatic substitution? (Two compounds shown: aniline with NH2, and anilinium ion with NH3+). B) Which carbonyl compound is more susceptible to nucleophilic attack? (Two compounds shown: cyclohexanone and hexanal). C) What is the purpose of the FeBr3 catalyst in an electrophilic aromatic substitution halogenation? i. It serves as a radical initiator ii. It destabilizes the carbocation intermediate iii. It acts as a Lewis acid to activate Br2. D) Rank in order of reactivity for electrophilic aromatic substitution (1 = most reactive): (four compounds shown) benzene, chlorobenzene, acetanilide (methyl amide attached to benzene), acetanilide derivative with NH-COCH3, with boxes for ranking. E) Rank in order of acidity (1 = most acidic): (four compounds shown) phenol, 4-nitrophenol, 3-nitrophenol, 4-aminophenol, with boxes for ranking.
canonical_answer: A) aniline (NH2-substituted benzene); B) hexanal (aldehyde); C) iii; D) 1=acetanilide derivative with NH-COCH3, 2=benzene, 3=chlorobenzene, 4=acetanilide; E) 1=4-nitrophenol, 2=3-nitrophenol, 3=phenol, 4=4-aminophenol

question_text: Draw the structure of the MAJOR product of the following reactions. If there is no reaction, write "NR". A) Reaction of a tert-butyl substituted benzene with HNO3/H2SO4. B) Reaction of benzene with isopropyl chloride in the presence of AlCl3. C) Reaction of 1-chloro-2,4-dinitrobenzene with piperidine. D) Reduction of a six-membered lactone with LiAlH4.
canonical_answer: A) tert-butylbenzene nitrated at the least sterically hindered ortho/para position with nitro group; B) isopropylbenzene formed via Friedel-Crafts alkylation with possible rearrangement; C) no aromatic substitution product, nucleophilic aromatic substitution with piperidine on dinitro-chlorobenzene; D) reduction of lactone to 1,6-hexanediol

error_comment: Task requires drawing and is not text-oriented

question_text: Provide the necessary reagents for the following transformations. More than one step may be required. A) Cyclopentanone to cyclopentylamine (amine group on cyclopentane). B) Benzene to 1-phenyl-1-propanol (benzene ring attached to a two-carbon chain with OH group). C) 1-butanol to butanoic acid. D) Cyclohexanol to cyclohexanone (cyclohexane ring with aldehyde group).
canonical_answer: A) 1) NH2CH2CH2CH3 2) NaBH4 or LiAlH4; B) 1) CH3CH2COCl, AlCl3 2) NaBH4 or LiAlH4; C) Na2Cr2O7, H2SO4; D) PCC

question_text: The equilibrium constant for hydration of acetone is 2 x 10^-3, which translates into a small percent conversion to the following hydrate: acetone (CH3-CO-CH3) equilibrates to the gem-diol hydrate (CH3-C(OH)2-CH3). The equilibrium constant Keq = 2 x 10^-3. Would you expect the equilibrium constant for hexafluoroacetone (F3C-CO-CF3) hydrate (F3C-C(OH)2-CF3) to be smaller or larger than that for acetone? Options: Keq greater than 2 x 10^-3; Keq less than 2 x 10^-3

Task requires drawing and is not text-oriented

question_text: Draw a synthesis of the following product from the given starting materials (additional reagents will be necessary). Each step in your synthesis must be clearly shown, but no mechanism is required. Product: cyclohexene with a propyl substituent attached to the double bond. Starting materials: cyclohexanone and 1-bromopropane. Handwritten notes mention Grignard followed by elimination and Wittig synthesis is the best (most regioselective) synthesis. Reaction scheme sketched in the answer: 1-bromopropane converted with PPh3 to propyltriphenylphosphonium salt, followed by treatment with n-BuLi to give the ylide, which then reacts with cyclohexanone to give the desired product.
canonical_answer: Convert 1-bromopropane to propyltriphenylphosphonium salt using PPh3; treat with n-BuLi to form the ylide; react with cyclohexanone to form the product by Wittig reaction

question_text: Furan is readily acetylated with acetic anhydride and a Lewis acid such as BF3. Predict the major regioisomer produced in this reaction and justify your reasoning with resonance structures. The reaction scheme shows furan reacting with acetic anhydride in the presence of BF3 to give an acetylated furan compound. Also consider resonance structures showing different placements of positive charge and substituent E on the furan ring.
canonical_answer: The major regioisomer is the 2-acetylfuran product, favored due to resonance stabilization involving three resonance structures with substitution at the 2-position, as shown by the resonance forms stabilizing the carbocation intermediate.
